originalBehavior: The published article loads through a route-local HTML shell plus shared CSS, GSAP, Pixi, allocator scripts, and many inline memory demos mounted across one longform page.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published article structure, route-local CSS, GSAP, Pixi, allocator scripts, and simulation runtime while rewriting only the subpath-hosting metadata, asset paths, and shared-footer provenance hook.
notes: The public body no longer exposes the upstream logo link, newsletter footer, social links, or other author-promo surfaces.
The route keeps the original article structure instead of condensing the allocator walkthrough into a custom rewrite.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the published article shell, route-local GSAP handoff, Pixi runtime, and allocator fetch paths as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the local scripts load, allocator-backed demos hydrate, and the public route stays responsive without upstream runtime dependencies.

originalBehavior: The published article opens with static and timeline-driven memory grids, then introduces hexadecimal addressing through a slider that keeps decimal and hexadecimal readouts synchronized.
localStatus: The local route keeps the opening memory-grid sequence and the hexadecimal slider scene under the original published runtime.
notes: The Haskie callouts remain in place with route-local image assets.
The hexadecimal slider still updates both the decimal and hexadecimal labels directly from the vendored runtime code.
evidence: The route-specific smoke contract includes the hexadecimal slider as an explicit synchronized-readout check.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the opening article initializes and the hexadecimal labels update after slider input.

originalBehavior: The published article fetches allocator scripts at runtime and uses them to drive slider-based timeline demos for stack, free-list, segmented, and inline allocation strategies.
localStatus: The local route keeps the allocator-backed demos intact, with the published runtime fetching route-local allocator scripts under the same relative shape expected by the source article.
notes: The route preserves the published next and previous controls, keyboard stepping, and slider-driven timeline state instead of replacing them with local UI.
Allocator fetch paths are localized so the demos no longer depend on the upstream root path.
evidence: Implementation planning treated at least one allocator-backed timeline demo as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies a hydrated memory simulation responds to slider input and that route-local allocator assets load without failed requests.

originalBehavior: The published article closes with a playground cross-link and an upstream footer that includes newsletter, social, and author provenance surfaces.
localStatus: The local route keeps the article close intact while leaving the shared footer as the only public original-page reference surface and neutralizing the non-local playground link in body copy.
notes: The route does not require upstream asset URLs or author-promo widgets at runtime.
The body-level playground link is converted to plain text because that upstream route is not shipped locally in this batch.
evidence: The public-surface audit is extended for the Sam Rose family so creator, share, and newsletter links are caught outside the shared footer and docs.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route stays runtime-clean while the footer remains the only provenance surface.
